name: I asked ChatGPT about my car accident case and it gave me advice - can I get in trouble for using that in court?
description: You won't be sanctioned for asking ChatGPT, but filing its unverified outputs risks penalties like in NY cases with fake citations.[2]

mainEntity.name: I asked ChatGPT about my car accident case and it gave me advice - can I get in trouble for using that in court?

acceptedAnswer.text: You won't be sanctioned for asking ChatGPT, but filing its unverified outputs risks penalties like in NY cases with fake citations.[2]
acceptedAnswer.description: No, you won't get sanctioned just for asking ChatGPT about your case, but submitting unverified AI-generated content like fake case law can lead to penalties.[2] Courts have sanctioned lawyers in New York for filing briefs with fabricated citations from ChatGPT, as in the 2023 Peter LoDuca case where nonexistent cases were cited.[2] Key factors: AI 'hallucinations' create fake legal references; always verify outputs; prompts and AI documents may be discoverable in litigation.[4][5] Consult a licensed attorney immediately to review any AI-assisted materials before filing; do not rely on ChatGPT for legal filings. Human lawyers provide verified advice essential for court.
